feat: implement API interface redesign (Phase 2)

New Features:
- ✅ Official Gradio Client Methods: predict() static method
- ✅ Enhanced Input Parameter Processing: support for TTS, Chat, and generic formats
- ✅ Flexible File Handling: improved handleGradioFile() with mime type detection
- ✅ API Endpoint Validation: validateApiEndpoint() for better error handling
- ✅ Cleaner Architecture: refactored main execute method to use predict()

Improvements:
- Automatic parameter normalization for different Gradio app types
- Better error messages and validation
- Support for multiple input formats (array, object, specialized)
- Enhanced file handling with proper mime type detection
- Configuration validation before API calls

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Accept both array format [123, "www"] and object format {"p1": 123, "p2": "www"}
- Object inputs are automatically wrapped in array for Gradio API compatibility
- Update placeholder and description to reflect new input options
- Bump version to 0.1.11

feat: major refactor - implement EventSource-based streaming (Phase 1.1-1.3)

BREAKING CHANGES:
- Replace HTTP polling with server-sent events processing
- Remove problematic session_hash management
- Implement proper Gradio API compliance
- Add enhanced error handling for different HTTP status codes
- Improve SSE parsing with event type awareness

Features:
- ✅ EventSource-based streaming pattern
- ✅ Session management fix
- ✅ Event-driven architecture foundation
- ✅ Better error messages for auth, rate limits, and API issues

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Stream OSLog output directly to VS Code Integrated Terminal

This commit refactors the OSLog integration to stream logs directly into a VS Code Integrated Terminal instead of an Output Channel, based on your feedback. The previous Output Channel implementation for OSLog has been removed.

Key changes:
- Introduced `OSLogTerminalManager` to manage the lifecycle of dedicated terminals for OSLog streaming.
- Implemented `selectOSLogTarget` UI to allow you to choose between:
    - Connected iOS devices.
    - Local macOS (with a default predicate for errors/faults).
    - Local macOS (with a user-provided custom predicate).
- Added new VS Code commands:
    - `sweetpad.osLogToTerminal.start`: Prompts for a target and starts streaming its OSLog to a new terminal.
    - `sweetpad.osLogToTerminal.stop`: Stops the currently active OSLog terminal stream.
- Updated `package.json` to reflect these new commands and removed old OSLog-related contributions.
- Updated `docs/wiki/oslog.md` to describe the new terminal-based OSLog streaming feature.
- Removed the previous `OSLogMonitor` (which used Output Channels) and its associated commands and configuration.

This approach provides you with a more conventional terminal interface for viewing live OSLog data, allowing for easier interaction and fitting better with common developer workflows for log analysis.
